headline: Jailed Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu condemns 'the autocracy that manufactured this unfair trial'
description: Over a year ago, Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu's arrest triggered a wave of protests across Turkey. He is seen as President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main opponent. Facing up to 2,352 years in prison, he has used his trial to denounce the regime's authoritarian drift and embody hope for political change in 2028.

headline: First Ebola case confirmed in DR Congo, WHO declares international health emergency

'Macron is paving the way for the return of African and Asian artifacts from French museums'
description: The unanimous adoption by France's parliament in early May of the law on the restitution of cultural property to foreign countries is a remarkable achievement in times of identity tensions, says Michel Guerrin, editor-in-chief at Le Monde, in his column.

headline: USS Gerald Ford aircraft carrier returns home after 326-day deployment
description: The largest aircraft carrier in the world, it was the longest deployment for a US carrier strike group since the Vietnam War.

headline: Elon Musk criticizes Christopher Nolan for casting Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy
description: The billionaire accused the filmmaker of 'defiling Homer' by choosing a Black actress, claiming Nolan only did so to comply with diversity requirements and win an Oscar. The controversy echoes the Trump administration's opposition to affirmative action policies.

headline: Inside a Texas migrant detention center: 'They treat us like animals'
description: At the border between the US and Mexico, federal immigration authorities have opened a massive detention site with 5,000 beds on the military grounds of Fort Bliss. A former detainee recounts the hell she endured behind those walls.

How Corsica's schools became the epicenter of a chess revolution
description: On the Île de Beauté in Corsica, chess players began learning the game since they were children, with the island counting 16 times more registered players per capita than the rest of France.

headline: How China is cracking down on its Christians
description: Le Monde verified footage from Chinese social media and collected testimonies from victims of Beijing's crackdown to reveal how the regime has brought independent churches to heel since Xi Jinping came to power.

headline: Military researcher Céline Marangé on the Ukraine war: 'What is truly in danger is the future of European liberal democracy'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the political scientist places the conflict in Ukraine within the long history of Russian imperialism and assesses the threat that the Kremlin poses to NATO and EU countries.
